attacking_style: Prefers extremely long-range attacks (35-50km from finish) on sustained climbs, showing exceptional confidence in aerobic power rather than explosive finishes. Struggles on steep, punchy gradients requiring acceleration.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of team support; appears to operate independently with emphasis on early solo moves rather than waiting for organized leadouts or positioning trains.
terrain_preference: Sustained, moderate gradient climbs in stage races and hilly Classics. Exposed on steep explosive walls (Mur de Huy) and when facing repeated attacks from elite GC riders.
vulnerability: Cannot respond when attacked by top-tier climbers; immediately distanced when pace escalates. Lacks finishing punch on steepest sections despite strong positioning. Defense of leads is passive.
key_patterns: Attacks ridiculously early 35-50km out in multiple Grand Tour stages, showing elite aerobic capacity.
Strong Ardennes week form across multiple Monuments (Amstel, Fleche, Liege) with consistent near-podium results.
Positioned well inside final kilometer but fades on steepest gradients requiring explosive power.
Cannot follow or respond to attacks from elite stage racers; immediately dropped when pace increases.
Willing to attack repeatedly after previous efforts, showing exceptional recovery and aggressive mentality.
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong early 2023 Monument performances and Giro stage win, but Tour de Suisse showed clear gap to elite level remains
